provenance.source_corpus: Effects of reforestation on near‐surface saturated hydraulic conductivity in a managed forest landscape, southern Ontario, Canada

source_spans.text: Reforestation of previously deforested landscapes often increases soil organic matter content and porosity as the plantation forest ages, leading to increased soil saturated hydraulic conductivity (KH).

Reforestation of previously deforested landscapes often increases soil organic matter content and porosity as the plantation forest ages, leading to increased soil saturated hydraulic conductivity (KH).
This recovery may be assisted by management activities such as thinning of red pine stands, which promotes understory development.
This recovery may be assisted by management activities such as thinning of red pine stands, which promotes understory development.
There was a statistically significant increase in mean KH (rs =0 /C1 50)... with forest age

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:e5b0dc59e3b5
edges.original_sentence: Temporal trends in KH were associated with increased understory density with forest age, suggesting that increases inKH were driven by understory root development and greater macroporosity.
